channel.title: GitHub Trending - Nuovi in JavaScript (Monthly)
channel.description: Repository (Nuovi) più popolari in JavaScript nel periodo monthly.

item.title: 📜 NVIDIA/NemoClaw (17668 ⭐)
item.description:
👤 Owner: NVIDIA

📝 Repository: NVIDIA/NemoClaw

📄 Descrizione: Run OpenClaw more securely inside NVIDIA OpenShell with managed inference

⭐ Stelle: 17668

⏰ Creato il: 2026-03-15T17:04:09Z

]]>

item.title: 📜 fjb040911/ai-rules (1101 ⭐)
item.description:
👤 Owner: fjb040911

📝 Repository: fjb040911/ai-rules

📄 Descrizione: ai-rules is a governance framework designed to solve "Architectural Decay" in AI-driven development. It forces AI Agents (Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot) to respect your project's boundaries, UI libraries, and design patterns.

⭐ Stelle: 1101

🍴 Forks: 18

💻 Linguaggio: JavaScript

🏷️ Topics: ai-code-review ai-coding code-review code-rules repair-prompt

item.title: 📜 cosmiciron/vmprint (655 ⭐)
item.description:
👤 Owner: cosmiciron

📝 Repository: cosmiciron/vmprint

📄 Descrizione: An alternative to React-PDF — render multi-page documents anywhere. Preview on canvas and export to PDF/SVG from the same layout — no PDF.js, no React, no server. Pure JS, no dependencies. ~1.7MB. https://cosmiciron.github.io/vmprint/examples/preview/

⭐ Stelle: 655

🍴 Forks: 25

💻 Linguaggio: JavaScript

🏷️ Topics: canvas document headless-browser javascript layout-engine multilingual pdf pdf-export pdf-generation pdf-viewer pdfjs publishing puppeteer react-pdf text-rendering typescript typesetting typst

📜 Licenza: Apache License 2.0

⏰ Creato il: 2026-03-01T11:29:14Z

🔄 Ultimo Aggiornamento: 2026-03-30T04:05:25Z

Visita il Repository su GitHub

]]>

item.title: 📜 SethGammon/Citadel (409 ⭐)
item.description:
👤 Owner: SethGammon

📝 Repository: SethGammon/Citadel

📄 Descrizione: Agent orchestration harness for Claude Code. Four-tier routing (/do), campaign persistence across sessions, parallel agents in isolated worktrees, discovery relay between waves, lifecycle hooks, circuit breaker, and 6 production-quality skills. From solo developer to institutional scale.
